commonIssues: Frost-sensitive annual: plants are killed by even a light frost, so sow only after the last frost and once soil is at least 16 C (60 F) at 10 cm depth (Clemson HGIC).
Direct-sow rather than transplant; beans resent root disturbance (Old Farmer's Almanac, UMN Extension).
Avoid high-nitrogen feed: too much nitrogen produces lush foliage and few pods. Beans fix their own nitrogen and have low fertilizer needs (UMN Extension, Old Farmer's Almanac).
Most critical watering period is during flowering, pod set, and pod development; drought stress then sharply reduces yield (Clemson HGIC, UMN Extension).
Container minimum (8 L) is sized for ONE bush plant; a 19 L (5-gallon) pot holds about three bush plants (Old Farmer's Almanac).

POLE/climbing varieties of the same species are NOT covered by the bush defaults here: they reach ~2 m, require a sturdy 1.8-2.4 m (6-8 ft) trellis or wigwam, and need a larger container (RHS: min ~75 cm wide x 45 cm deep). For pole beans set requiresSupport=true and use a larger pot.

source.citation: University of Minnesota Extension - Growing beans in home gardens. Bush beans grow ~2 ft tall and do not need support; pole beans need a trellis and can reach 6 ft. One inch of rainfall per week; an inch of water wets sandy soil to ~10 in and clay to ~6 in. Beans need less fertilizer than other crops; prefers pH 6-7. Plant after soil warms (late May / early June in MN).

University of Minnesota Extension - Growing beans in home gardens. Bush beans do not need support (requiresSupport=false); seeds spaced ~4 in apart, rows 2-3 ft apart. Beans require less fertilizer than most crops (low feeding need).

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC 1308 - Bush & Pole-Type Snap Beans. Snap beans grow best at average temperatures of 60-75 F; do not plant until soil is at least 60 F at 4-in depth; plants are killed by light frost (frostSensitive). Maturity ~55 days. Pre-plant fertilizer 5-10-10; sidedress nitrogen before first bloom. Water to moisten soil to 6-in depth; critical moisture period is pod set and pod development.

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC 1308 - Bush & Pole-Type Snap Beans. Recommended fertilizer 5-10-10 pre-plant with a nitrogen sidedress before first bloom; supports the low-nitrogen, ~30-day feeding cadence.

The Old Farmer's Almanac - How to Grow Green Beans. Beans need full sun; container guidance ~6-8 hours of sun per day. An 8-in deep x 8-in wide container holds one bush plant; a 5-gallon (~19 L) pot holds up to three bush or pole plants. Use a low-nitrogen 5-10-10 to avoid excess foliage. Direct-sow after frost into warm (55 F+) soil.
The Old Farmer's Almanac - How to Grow Green Beans. Full sun; container beans want roughly 6-8 hours of direct sun per day (vegetables grown for pods perform best with 8+ hours).

Royal Horticultural Society - How to grow French beans. Highly frost-sensitive; sow outdoors only after the last frost. Dwarf (bush) varieties ~45 cm tall need only twiggy support; climbing varieties ~2 m need tall canes/wigwams. Dwarf-bean container min ~30-45 cm wide; climbing-bean container min ~75 cm wide x 45 cm deep. Water during dry weather, especially once flowering and forming pods.

Confidence note: heatStressC (29 C / ~85 F) is a conservative estimate derived from Clemson's stated optimal range of 60-75 F (above which bean blossoms commonly drop) rather than a single published heat-stress threshold. coldHardyMinC (4 C) is likewise conservative: sources state plants are killed by light frost, so the safe lower bound is set above freezing rather than at an exact published figure. hotWeatherMultiplier (1.5) and rainSkipThresholdMm (25 mm = the cited 1 in/week) are derived operational defaults, not directly published intervals.
